subject: Project Management
title: Strategic Misrepresentation
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Strategic Misrepresentation.

hook.text: Some projects aren't underestimated by accident. They're underestimated on purpose.

buildup.text: Project sponsors lowball costs and timelines to get approval. Once approved, the truth gradually emerges.

discovery.text: Flyvbjerg calls this 'strategic misrepresentation' — deliberate deception to secure funding.

twist.text: It's rational for individuals: lie to win the project, then manage the overrun later.

climax.text: Strategic misrepresentation is the dark twin of the planning fallacy — one is innocent, the other calculated.

punchline.text: Sometimes the budget isn't wrong. It's a lie.

quiz.question: What is strategic misrepresentation?
options: Deliberately underestimating costs to get project approval
Exaggerating costs to get more funding
